question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a cryptographic integrity check on the jdbcUrl to perform a configuration file tampering attack (e.g., modifying application.yml in a shared filesystem), redirecting the JDBC connection to a rogue database that serves manipulated transaction history with altered consensus_timestamp ordering?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/util/DatabaseWaiter.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: waitForDatabase()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the connectTimeout and socketTimeout are converted via toSeconds() (losing sub-second precision) to cause the JDBC driver to use a longer-than-intended timeout, creating a window during which a slow rogue database can inject manipulated transaction ordering data before the connection is closed?
